# 📤 Backend: Data Export & Reporting System

## Overview
Enable users to export analytics, referrals, connections, and other data in multiple formats (CSV, PDF, Excel, JSON) with scheduled reports and secure sharing capabilities.

## Priority
**Low-Medium** - Nice-to-have enhancement

## Tech Stack
- NestJS service
- CSV generation (papaparse or csv-writer)
- PDF generation (PDFKit or Puppeteer)
- Excel generation (ExcelJS)
- Bull queue for async processing
- AWS S3 or similar for report storage

---

## Task 1: Export Service Architecture

### Core Export Service
- [ ] Create `ExportService` with modular architecture
- [ ] Design export job queue system
- [ ] Implement format-specific generators (CSV, PDF, Excel, JSON)
- [ ] Add export tracking and history
- [ ] Set up file storage (local or cloud)

### Database Schema
```prisma
model ExportJob {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId        String
  user          User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  exportType    String   // ANALYTICS, REFERRALS, CONNECTIONS, CUSTOM
  format        String   // CSV, PDF, EXCEL, JSON
  filters       Json?    // Export parameters
  status        String   // PENDING, PROCESSING, COMPLETED, FAILED
  fileUrl       String?
  fileSize      Int?
  expiresAt     DateTime?
  error         String?
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  completedAt   DateTime?
  
  @@index([userId, createdAt])
  @@index([status, createdAt])
}

model ScheduledReport {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId        String
  user          User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id])
  name          String
  reportType    String   // ANALYTICS_SUMMARY, REFERRAL_REPORT, etc.
  format        String
  schedule      String   // Cron expression
  filters       Json?
  recipients    String[] // Email addresses
  enabled       Boolean  @default(true)
  lastRunAt     DateTime?
  nextRunAt     DateTime
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  
  @@index([userId, enabled, nextRunAt])
}
```

### API Endpoints
```
POST /export/request
  Body: { type, format, filters }
  Response: { jobId, estimatedTime }

GET /export/status/:jobId
GET /export/download/:jobId
GET /export/history?userId=X
DELETE /export/:jobId
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Supports all export types and formats
- [ ] Jobs process asynchronously
- [ ] Export history tracked per user
- [ ] File cleanup after expiration

---

## Task 2: CSV Export Implementation

### Data Types to Export
- [ ] Analytics dashboard data
- [ ] Connections list with metadata
- [ ] Referrals and applications
- [ ] Mentorship sessions
- [ ] User profile data
- [ ] Community membership

### CSV Features
- [ ] Column selection/customization
- [ ] UTF-8 encoding support
- [ ] Large dataset pagination
- [ ] Data sanitization (remove HTML, etc.)

### Implementation
```typescript
interface CSVExportOptions {
  includeHeaders: boolean;
  delimiter: string; // comma, semicolon, tab
  encoding: string;  // utf-8, utf-16
  columns?: string[]; // Selected columns
}
```

### Sample Exports
**Connections Export:**
```csv
Name,Email,Role,Graduation Year,Connection Date,Status
John Doe,john@example.com,ALUMNI,2020,2025-01-01,ACCEPTED
...
```

**Referrals Export:**
```csv
Job Title,Company,Location,Posted Date,Applications,Status
Senior Engineer,Google,Remote,2025-01-01,15,APPROVED
...
```

### API Endpoints
```
POST /export/csv/analytics
POST /export/csv/connections
POST /export/csv/referrals
POST /export/csv/custom
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CSV exports are valid and parseable
- [ ] Handles large datasets (10k+ rows)
- [ ] Encoding issues resolved
- [ ] Opens correctly in Excel/Google Sheets

---

## Task 3: PDF Export Implementation

### PDF Report Types
- [ ] Analytics summary report
- [ ] Referral performance report
- [ ] Network growth report
- [ ] Custom filtered reports

### PDF Features
- [ ] Professional template with branding
- [ ] Charts and visualizations embedded
- [ ] Page numbers and table of contents
- [ ] Watermark for confidential data
- [ ] Print-friendly formatting

### Template Design
```
Page 1: Cover page
  - Report title
  - Date range
  - Generated by
  - Logo and branding

Page 2-N: Content
  - Summary statistics
  - Charts (exported as images)
  - Tables with data
  - Insights and recommendations

Last Page: Footer
  - Disclaimer
  - Export timestamp
  - Confidentiality notice
```

### Implementation Options
1. **PDFKit** (lightweight, programmatic)
2. **Puppeteer** (HTML→PDF, more flexibility)

### API Endpoints
```
POST /export/pdf/analytics-report
POST /export/pdf/referral-report
POST /export/pdf/network-report
POST /export/pdf/custom
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PDFs are professional and readable
- [ ] Charts render correctly
- [ ] File size optimized (<5MB)
- [ ] Print layout is correct

---

## Task 4: Excel Export Implementation

### Excel Features
- [ ] Multiple sheets per workbook
- [ ] Formatted cells (colors, borders)
- [ ] Formulas for calculations
- [ ] Charts embedded in sheets
- [ ] Freeze panes for headers

### Workbook Structure
```
Sheet 1: Summary
  - Key metrics
  - Period comparison
  
Sheet 2: Detailed Data
  - Full dataset
  - Sortable columns
  
Sheet 3: Charts
  - Embedded visualizations
  
Sheet 4: Notes
  - Export details
  - Data dictionary
```

### Implementation (ExcelJS)
```typescript
const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
const sheet = workbook.addWorksheet('Analytics');

// Add headers
sheet.addRow(['Metric', 'Value', 'Change']);

// Format headers
sheet.getRow(1).font = { bold: true };
sheet.getRow(1).fill = { 
  type: 'pattern', 
  pattern: 'solid', 
  fgColor: { argb: 'FF4A90E2' } 
};

// Add data
data.forEach(row => sheet.addRow(row));

// Add chart
const chart = sheet.addChart(...);
```

### API Endpoints
```
POST /export/excel/analytics
POST /export/excel/referrals
POST /export/excel/connections
POST /export/excel/custom
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Excel files open without errors
- [ ] Formulas calculate correctly
- [ ] Charts display properly
- [ ] File size reasonable (<10MB)

---

## Task 5: JSON Export Implementation

### JSON Features
- [ ] Structured, nested data
- [ ] Include metadata
- [ ] Pretty-print option
- [ ] Schema documentation

### JSON Structure
```json
{
  "export": {
    "type": "analytics",
    "format": "json",
    "version": "1.0",
    "generatedAt": "2026-01-06T10:00:00Z",
    "userId": "user-id",
    "period": {
      "start": "2025-01-01",
      "end": "2025-12-31"
    }
  },
  "data": {
    "summary": { ... },
    "connections": [ ... ],
    "analytics": { ... }
  },
  "metadata": {
    "totalRecords": 1250,
    "fieldsIncluded": ["name", "email", "..."]
  }
}
```

### Use Cases
- API integration
- Data migration
- Backup purposes
- Advanced analysis tools

### API Endpoints
```
POST /export/json/full-profile
POST /export/json/analytics
POST /export/json/custom
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] JSON is valid and well-formed
- [ ] Schema is documented
- [ ] File size optimized
- [ ] Supports nested data structures

---

## Task 6: Scheduled Reports

### Scheduling Features
- [ ] Daily, weekly, monthly schedules
- [ ] Custom cron expressions
- [ ] Multiple recipients per report
- [ ] Email delivery with attachment
- [ ] Retry logic for failures

### Implementation
```typescript
interface ScheduledReportConfig {
  name: string;
  type: ReportType;
  format: ExportFormat;
  schedule: string;  // Cron: "0 9 * * 1" (Every Monday at 9am)
  recipients: string[];
  filters?: ReportFilters;
}
```

### CRON Job
- [ ] Check for due reports every 5 minutes
- [ ] Generate report
- [ ] Send email with attachment or download link
- [ ] Update `lastRunAt` and `nextRunAt`
- [ ] Log execution and errors

### Email Template
```
Subject: Your Scheduled Report: {Report Name}

Hi {User Name},

Your scheduled report "{Report Name}" is ready.

Report Period: {Start Date} - {End Date}
Generated: {Timestamp}

[Download Report] (Link expires in 7 days)

Summary:
- Total Connections: 150 (+10 from last week)
- Referrals Applied: 5
- Engagement Score: 85

Thanks,
Nexus Team
```

### API Endpoints
```
POST /export/schedule
GET /export/schedules?userId=X
PUT /export/schedule/:id
DELETE /export/schedule/:id
POST /export/schedule/:id/test (Send now)
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reports generate on schedule
- [ ] Emails deliver reliably
- [ ] Failed jobs retry automatically
- [ ] Users can manage their schedules

---

## Task 7: Secure Sharing & Access Control

### Sharing Features
- [ ] Generate shareable links
- [ ] Optional password protection
- [ ] Expiration dates
- [ ] View-only access
- [ ] Track who accessed

### Database Schema
```prisma
model SharedExport {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  exportJobId   String
  exportJob     ExportJob @relation(fields: [exportJobId], references: [id])
  shareToken    String   @unique
  password      String?  // Hashed
  expiresAt     DateTime?
  maxViews      Int?
  viewCount     Int      @default(0)
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now())
  accessLog     Json[]   // [{accessedAt, ip, userAgent}]
  
  @@index([shareToken])
}
```

### Security Features
- [ ] Unique, non-guessable tokens (UUID or similar)
- [ ] Optional password protection (bcrypt hashed)
- [ ] Rate limiting on access attempts
- [ ] IP logging for audit trail
- [ ] Auto-delete after expiration

### API Endpoints
```
POST /export/share/:jobId
  Body: { password?, expiresIn?, maxViews? }
  Response: { shareUrl, token }

GET /export/shared/:token
  Query: ?password=xxx
  
DELETE /export/shared/:token
GET /export/shared/:token/stats (Owner only)
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shared links are secure
- [ ] Password protection works
- [ ] Expiration is enforced
- [ ] Access logs are accurate

---

## Task 8: Background Processing & Optimization

### Async Job Processing
- [ ] Use Bull queue for heavy exports
- [ ] Provide real-time progress updates
- [ ] Cancel running jobs
- [ ] Job prioritization

### Progress Tracking
```typescript
interface ExportProgress {
  jobId: string;
  status: 'queued' | 'processing' | 'completed' | 'failed';
  progress: number; // 0-100
  currentStep: string;
  estimatedTimeRemaining: number; // seconds
}
```

### Optimizations
- [ ] Stream large datasets (don't load all in memory)
- [ ] Compress exports (gzip)
- [ ] Cache frequently requested exports
- [ ] Parallel processing for multi-sheet exports

### API Endpoints
```
GET /export/progress/:jobId
POST /export/cancel/:jobId
GET /export/queue-status (Admin only)
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Jobs don't block server
- [ ] Progress updates in real-time
- [ ] Can export 100k+ records
- [ ] Memory usage stays reasonable
